description: Synthetic HOLDOUT mutation mapped to the money-payments lens: a currency amount is handled as a float instead of integer cents, so rounding drifts. The money-payments lens must catch it.

evidence_trace: src/billing/charge.ts:29 computes `total = price * 1.075` in floating point
the result is passed to the charge API without rounding to integer cents
float arithmetic on money accumulates rounding error across line items
finding.failure_scenario: Float arithmetic on the charge amount drifts by a cent, so the customer is charged an amount that disagrees with the invoice.
finding.model_authored_prose: This charge amount is computed in floating point, which drifts on cents. Compute in integer cents (or a decimal type) and round explicitly before charging.

prContext.title: billing: simplify tax computation in chargeCustomer
prContext.description: Replaces the basis-points integer math with a plain rate for readability.
prContext.author: dev-billing
